use_classification: Class 323: Shopping Centers (official MA assessor use code) -- corroborates property_type=retail/shopping_center classification.
resolution_method: owner-entity aggregation (grouped all parcels owned by the legal entity, from street address anchor)

total_building_sqft.method: sum of Springfield assessor record-card Interior/Exterior area tables, all 9 parcels, all building cards, LAND-type cards excluded
total_building_sqft.source_system: Springfield Assessor commercial property record cards

building.verdict: Building size confirmed by 3 independent sources (1 government, 2 private marketing) within normal measurement-convention tolerance.
building.source_note: Grade/features from Springfield assessor record cards, main parcel (7 buildings, 93% of total sqft) + 2 outparcels; remaining small outparcels (400/416/REAR/W S Cooley, 1761/1785 Allen) not individually re-pulled for feature detail -- minor pads, low incremental value.

parent.name: Phillips Edison Grocery Center REIT I, Inc.
parent.note: later renamed/became Phillips Edison & Company, Inc., NASDAQ: PECO

parent_chain.source: SEC Exhibit 21.1 (Subsidiaries of the Registrant), filed with Phillips Edison Grocery Center REIT I FY2014 Form 10-K
parent_chain.note: Entity naming pattern "[Property Name] Station LLC" appears ~150+ times in this same exhibit -- confirmed as PECO's standard one-subsidiary-per-property holding structure, not a one-off.

ownership.note: Formally confirmed via SEC Exhibit 21.1, not inferred from mailing address alone.
ownership.property_manager: Phillips Edison & Company (self-managed, per PECO's own property page)

transaction_history.structure: All-cash; part of Urstadt Biddle's tax-deferred reverse 1031 exchange (down-leg: 2 Greenwich CT properties, $47.4M)

transaction_history.note: Matches Urstadt Biddle's own account of owning the property 'since the early 1970s' -- Hubbard likely a predecessor/merged name, not verified as identical entity.
transaction_history.confidence: direct (assessor record), relationship to Urstadt Biddle inferred

financing.finding: No mortgage recorded against the parcel (confirmed via Hampden registry, cross-validated against a working scraper).
financing.explanation: Seller (Urstadt Biddle) SEC press release states the sale was "an all cash price" and that proceeds were used to repay Urstadt Biddle's own UNSECURED revolving credit facility -- consistent with no property-specific mortgage being recorded. Likely explanation: Phillips Edison financed the acquisition at the corporate/OP level (common for grocery-anchored shopping center REITs) rather than via a property-secured loan, which is why Hampden County shows no MTG document.
financing.explanation_confidence: estimated/inferred (fact_type=inferred) -- we did not find a PECO-side filing that explicitly states their financing method for this specific acquisition, only that it is consistent with the absence of a recorded mortgage.

foreclosure_bankruptcy_check.direct_court_search: NOT PERFORMED -- PACER (federal bankruptcy court) requires a paid account; not attempted.
foreclosure_bankruptcy_check.indirect_evidence: Our full Hampden County deeds pull (all document types, all years, all parties) returned no foreclosure-related instrument (Notice of Sale, Certificate of Entry, etc.) under this entity name. A real foreclosure event would very likely leave a recorded trace at the county level even before/alongside any federal bankruptcy filing.
foreclosure_bankruptcy_check.verdict: No evidence of foreclosure. Bankruptcy genuinely unchecked (would need PACER).

cross_validation.note: Ollies Bargain Outlet (space 30, 35,100 sqft) independently confirmed by TWO unrelated sources: this PECO leasing flyer AND a recorded Lease document at Hampden County Registry of Deeds dated 2025-11-19 (book/page 26117-468). Two independent authoritative sources agreeing = VERIFIED confidence tier per spec section 23.

tenants.source_confidence_note: fact_type=direct + value_confidence=0.9 (not 1.0) because this is the landlord/property-manager marketing collateral, not a government record -- slightly lower confidence than the assessor/registry sources, consistent with spec confidence rules (Strong, not Verified, unless cross-confirmed -- Ollies IS cross-confirmed, see cross_validation).

permits.source: Springfield Assessor record card, Permit Information section (main parcel only so far)
permits.note: This directly satisfies spec section 13 (Permit/Inspection/Code data) for the main parcel -- real permit_type/date/value records, not inferred.

data_quality_log.issue: year_built field polluted with raw HTML link in original Springfield assessor CSV export
data_quality_log.resolution: Fetched actual record cards; got real year_built per building (1970-2004) instead of the broken field.

data_quality_log.issue: Naive building-sqft sum initially overcounted by 22% (65,340 sqft)
data_quality_log.resolution: One 'card' on 1761 Allen St had Structure Type=LAND, not a building -- excluded after checking field semantics, not just summing numbers.

data_quality_log.issue: Hampden Registry of Deeds and MA Secretary of State portals rate-limited/blocked further automated queries
data_quality_log.resolution: Backed off rather than force; pivoted to SEC EDGAR (a better, unblocked, MORE authoritative source given the owner is a public REIT).

strong_tier: tenant roster (owner's own flyer, cross-confirmed on Ollie's specifically)
flood risk (FEMA zone X, all 9 parcels)
estimated_tier: explanation for absence of a recorded mortgage
annual tax bill (assessed value x public FY2026 rate)
unresolved: individual tenant lease effective-dates (flyer is undated)
whether other tenants besides Ollie's have registry-recorded leases
direct market comparables (needs paid CRE data source)

sources_used: Springfield Assessor (ArcGIS ownportal CSV, already in DealSynq outputs/)
Springfield Assessor commercial property record cards (assessors-search/assessors.php)
Hampden County Registry of Deeds (search.hampdendeeds.com, ALIS platform)
Phillips Edison & Company official leasing flyer
SEC EDGAR (Phillips Edison Grocery Center REIT I 10-K Exhibit 21.1; Urstadt Biddle Properties Form 8-K)

coordinates.source_system: Phillips Edison & Company official leasing flyer, page 1

demographics.source_system: Phillips Edison & Company official leasing flyer, page 1
demographics.note: Owner-published radius demographics, not an independent Census pull -- Strong tier, not Verified. A general 'demographics by lat/lon' step (Census API) would be the reusable version of this for properties whose owner doesn't publish a flyer.

hazard.flood_zone_meaning: Area of minimal flood hazard (outside the 500-year floodplain) per FEMA
hazard.source_system: Springfield Assessor CSV (FEMA flood-zone fields, already present in the statewide scrape -- outputs/springfield_ownportal.csv)
hazard.note: This field already existed in data collected months ago for the whole MA pipeline; it just hadn't been pulled into a property profile before now.

price_per_square_foot.note: Uses the 2014 acquisition price against current building sqft -- a historical-cost yardstick, not a current market-value-per-sqft (no current appraisal/BOV exists).

hold_period_years.note: Years since acquisition by current owner (Five Town Station LLC).

epa_regulated_facility_check.method: EPA ECHO facility-search REST API, queried all 42 EPA-regulated facilities in ZIP 01118
epa_regulated_facility_check.result: No facility registered at any of the 9 parcel addresses (380/270/296/400/416/REAR/W S Cooley St, 1761/1785 Allen St)

massdep_state_level_check.status: BLOCKED -- eeaonline.eea.state.ma.us waste-site portal is a JS-rendered Angular SPA, not scrapeable via plain HTTP. Would need browser automation to check state-level records (spills, USTs) not covered by the federal EPA check.

environmental.note: The car wash structure on one outparcel (416 Cooley St) is the one use-type on this property that would typically carry UST/wastewater considerations -- federal check is clean; state-level check unresolved.

code_violations_check.status: BLOCKED -- Springfield's code-enforcement portal (citizen.springfieldma.intelligovsoftware.com) requires an account login to search cases; no public read-only lookup found.

planning_applications.status: PARTIAL -- no searchable online case database found for Springfield Planning Board (meeting minutes only, not machine-readable). However, a 'Decision' document IS already on record from our deeds pull: 2017-01-06, grantor=Five Town Station LLC, counterparty=Springfield City, book/page 21523-581 -- very likely a planning/zoning decision, already captured, not re-verified against a case file.

market_context.note: Springfield trades at a wider (higher) cap rate than the national average -- consistent with a secondary-market classification, not a property-specific red flag. No direct comparable-sale set was gathered (would need a paid CRE data source e.g. CoStar/CBRE).

traffic_counts.source: PECO leasing flyer site plan (owner source)

location_context.source_system: Springfield Assessor CSV (fields already collected in the statewide MA scrape)
location_context.note: All 9 parcels share the same neighborhood/tract/sector. Historic/overlay/CDBG are real negative findings (blank/N in the source).

geometry.parcel_geometry_available: Yes — GeoJSON polygon for all 9 parcels (source: Springfield Assessor ownportal, already on disk)
geometry.note: Parcel outlines + centroids are in hand. Actual BUILDING footprint outlines (vs. parcel outlines) still require a computer-vision / footprint-dataset step (not free).

tax.source: Assessed value from Springfield Assessor; FY2026 CIP tax rate from MA DLS / City of Springfield
tax.note: Estimate: applies the citywide FY2026 commercial rate to total assessed value. Actual billed amount may differ slightly with exemptions/special assessments.

remaining_free_gaps_deferred: 1-mile radius demographics (needs Census block-group radius aggregation; 3-mi & 5-mi captured)
Permit history for the 8 non-main parcels (same record-card source; main parcel = 93% of value, captured)
